headline: US Government Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for All Foreign Nationals
summary: The US government issued an export control directive requiring Anthropic to immediately suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 — its two most capable models — for any foreign national, including Anthropic's own foreign national employees. Anthropic says the government believes a method exists for jailbreaking Fable 5, raising national security concerns. To ensure compliance Anthropic disabled both models for all customers, not just foreign nationals. Amazon's Andy Jassy reportedly flagged concerns to the White House that helped trigger the directive. Anthropic complied but publicly disputes the standard, warning it would halt all frontier model deployments industry-wide if broadly applied.

headline: 42 State AGs Launch Investigation into OpenAI Days After Its IPO Filing
summary: A coalition of 42 US state attorneys general, led by New York, served OpenAI with a subpoena on June 13 seeking documents on a wide range of topics: advertising, user engagement and retention, model sycophancy, consumer and health data handling, treatment of minors and seniors, and deep learning model design. The timing — less than a week after OpenAI's confidential S-1 filing at an $852B valuation — adds material legal risk to one of the largest upcoming IPOs in history. Sycophancy, a tendency for models to tell users what they want to hear rather than what is accurate, is explicitly named as a concern in the subpoena.

headline: KPMG Pulls AI Report After UBS, NHS, and Others Say Its Claims Were Fabricated
summary: KPMG retracted its report 'Redefining excellence in the age of agentic AI' after forensic review found that only 5 of its 45 citations correctly pointed to the cited source — the rest ranged from mangled to partially fabricated. GPTZero traced the inaccuracies to AI hallucinations. UBS, the UK's National Health Service, Swiss Federal Railways, and Transport for London all told the FT that claims made about their AI usage in the report were untrue or misleading. The incident has become a high-profile example of AI-generated research escaping quality control at a major professional services firm.
source: TechCrunch (also: The Next Web, The Register)

headline: AI Lab CEOs Join G7 Summit in France as Sovereign AI and Export Controls Take Center Stage
summary: Sam Altman (OpenAI), Dario Amodei (Anthropic), and Demis Hassabis (Google DeepMind) are all attending the G7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France, June 15–17 — the first G7 meeting with representation from all three major AI companies. French President Macron extended a personal invitation to Altman. The summit agenda positions AI sovereignty and export control standards as top priorities, with G7 allies reportedly divided over how to align national AI policies. The attendance comes as Anthropic and OpenAI are both in the midst of IPO processes and as the US government just invoked export authority to suspend Anthropic's frontier models.

headline: OpenAI Launches $150M Partner Network, Targets 300,000 Certified Consultants by Year-End
summary: OpenAI officially launched the OpenAI Partner Network on June 15, committing $150 million to build a global ecosystem of systems integrators, management consultants, and technology providers. The network uses a three-tier structure (Select, Advanced, Elite) and pilots a Forward Deployed Experts program that embeds qualified partner practitioners with OpenAI's own engineering teams on complex enterprise deployments. OpenAI is targeting 300,000 certified consultants by end of 2026 and plans to go live in July. Featured launch partnerships include BCG with Agilent, Bain with Paychex, and Accenture with T-Mobile.

headline: Anthropic Claude Agent SDK Billing Splits Today — Action Required if You Run Automated Pipelines
summary: Effective June 15, Anthropic separates automated usage (Claude Agent SDK, claude -p CLI, Claude Code GitHub Actions, third-party agents) from interactive Claude subscription usage into a distinct monthly credit pool: $20 for Pro, $100 for Max 5x, $200 for Max 20x. These credits are metered at full API rates, do not roll over, and have no automatic fallback — when the credit is exhausted, automated requests stop unless you have explicitly enabled overflow billing at API rates. Users need to claim their credit once through their Claude account and decide whether to enable usage credits for overflow.
foryou: If you're running any automated agentic pipelines through the Agent SDK or claude -p, you need to act today: claim your credit pool and enable overflow billing or your agents will silently fail at the end of your credit.

headline: Alteryx Agent Studio Enters Preview: Convert Existing Analytics Workflows Into Autonomous Agents
summary: Alteryx launched Agent Studio (now in preview) and an Alteryx One MCP Server at its Inspire 2026 conference, letting business analysts convert existing data workflows and business logic into autonomous AI agents without rewriting code. The MCP Server connects those agents to workplace tools like Slack and Microsoft Teams, and to LLMs including Claude and OpenAI, making Alteryx workflows callable tools any AI model can invoke via the Model Context Protocol. The core insight driving the product: the bottleneck to enterprise AI at scale is no longer model access but verified, trustworthy business context.
foryou: If you're building enterprise agentic systems and need to expose existing business logic as MCP-callable tools, Alteryx's server gives you a production-grade path that sidesteps the need to reimplement validated data workflows from scratch.
